subject: UX & Research
title: Revenue Per User and UX
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Revenue Per User and UX.

hook.text: Forrester found that every $1 invested in UX returns $100. Revenue per user measures whether that's true.

buildup.text: ARPU (average revenue per user) tracks how much each user spends. UX improvements should move this number.

discovery.text: When Amazon simplified one-click buying, ARPU jumped. Removing one friction step increased spending per user.

twist.text: Upselling dark patterns inflate ARPU short-term. Users who feel tricked buy once and never return.

climax.text: Track ARPU alongside satisfaction scores. Revenue growth without happy users is borrowed time.

punchline.text: Good UX grows revenue per user. Dark patterns steal it.

quiz.question: What ROI did Forrester Research find for UX investment?
options: UX investment has no measurable ROI
